calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT AGUSTIN FLORES' MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's motion to vacate the renewal of the judgment is granted.

"A successful motion under section 683.170 does not affect the validity of the default or the default judgment . . . A successful motion under section 683.170 vacates only the renewal of the judgment thereby precluding its extended enforceability under section 683.120." (Fidelity Creditor Service, Inc. v. Browne (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 195, 203-04.) Under Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 683.020, a "money judgment is enforceable for 10 years from the date of its entry." (Id. at 200.) A judgment creditor can extend the life of the judgment by renewing it for an additional 10 years, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 683.170. (Id.) The "complete failure to have ever served process on a defendant is a defense to an action on the judgment and . . . provides a basis for a motion to vacate a renewed judgment." (Id. at 197, 201-02 [holding that the "debtor's uncontradicted assertion that he was never served with a summons and complaint in the underlying action that resulted in the default judgment was ground for vacating renewal of that judgment."].)

"The judgment debtor bears the burden of proving, by a preponderance of the evidence, that he or she is entitled to relief under section 683.170." (Fidelity, 89 Cal.App.4th at 199.) Defendant meets his burden. Defendant declares that he "never received the Summons and Complaint for this case," that he was not home at the time the time of the stated service, and that the description of who was served is not of Defendant at all. (Flores Decl. Paras. 3, 8.) When a defendant argues that service of summons did not bring him or her within the trial court's jurisdiction, the plaintiff has 'the burden of proving the facts that did give the court jurisdiction, that is the facts requisite to an effective service." (Am. Express Centurion Bank v. Zara (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 383, 387.) Plaintiff fails to provide any factual evidence to contradict Defendant's declaration.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
